name: Ace of Cups
uprightMeaning: The heart's overflow. The Ace of Cups is pure emotional beginnings — love offered, grief released, joy awakened, an open heart.
reversedMeaning: Blocked emotions, emptiness. A heart closed — or a flood of feeling that cannot be contained.
symbolism: hand offering cup from which water overflows

name: Two of Cups
uprightMeaning: Two vessels, one flow. The Two of Cups signals mutual attraction and partnership — an exchange of equals, a connection forged in understanding.
reversedMeaning: Imbalanced relationship, tension. A connection that drains rather than fills.

name: Three of Cups
uprightMeaning: Three hearts aligned. Three of Cups marks celebration in community — friendship, creative collaboration, shared joy, belonging.
reversedMeaning: Overinvolvement, gossip, third party. The trio fractures — or outside influence disrupts the gathering.
symbolism: three women in a garden holding cups aloft

name: Four of Cups
uprightMeaning: Withdrawal from abundance. Four of Cups shows a state of disengagement — an offer declined, gifts unseen, the self absorbed in its own dissatisfaction.
reversedMeaning: Sudden awareness, choosing happiness. The veil lifts and what was present all along finally becomes visible.
symbolism: figure seated under a tree with three cups
hand reaching from cloud offering cup

name: Five of Cups
uprightMeaning: Spilled vessels, grief unhealed. Five of Cups fixates on what has been lost — while behind, two cups still stand full, untouched by the spill.
reversedMeaning: Acceptance, finding peace. The grief is honored but the spiral breaks — a choice to look toward what remains.
symbolism: figure in dark robe gazing at three spilled cups

name: Six of Cups
uprightMeaning: Return to the source. Six of Cups evokes memories of childhood purity — the comfort of familiar places, reunion with the past, sharing and innocence.
reversedMeaning: Living in the past, naivety. Memories are precious but cannot sustain the present.
symbolism: two children exchanging cups in a garden

name: Seven of Cups
uprightMeaning: The forest of options. Seven of Cups presents a bewildering array of choices — each a different fantasy, each seductive, most illusory.
reversedMeaning: Searching for truth, focus. A vision clarified — or the painful process of distinguishing real from illusion.
symbolism: figure before seven cups containing visions

name: Eight of Cups
uprightMeaning: The deliberate departure. Eight of Cups shows a figure walking away from what no longer nourishes — a cup left empty, a path chosen into solitude.
reversedMeaning: Fear of change, avoidance. Refusing to leave even when the path is clear — or running when stillness was needed.
symbolism: figure walking away from eight cups stacked

name: Nine of Cups
uprightMeaning: The wish fulfilled. Nine of Cups represents emotional satisfaction and abundance — a genuine state of gratitude, the heart at rest in plenty.
reversedMeaning: Dissatisfaction, greed. A heart that cannot be filled — or the realization that what was wished for does not satisfy.
symbolism: well-dressed figure with nine cups arranged in arc

name: Ten of Cups
uprightMeaning: The home raised in love. Ten of Cups shows a family beneath a rainbow of cups — domestic bliss, shared values, a community in alignment.
reversedMeaning: Broken home, misalignment. Family bonds strained or broken — or the courage to leave a false home.
symbolism: family beneath rainbow with ten cups

name: Page of Cups
uprightMeaning: An emotional messenger. The Page of Cups arrives bearing intuitive tidings — the first stirrings of a feeling that demands attention, a creative whisper.
reversedMeaning: Self-limiting beliefs, escapism. Creative dreams deferred or distorted by fear.
symbolism: figure holding cup with fish emerging

name: Knight of Cups
uprightMeaning: Riding the emotional current. The Knight of Cups moves with the heart's desire — persuasive, romantic, sometimes impractical, always sincere.
reversedMeaning: Moodiness, impracticality, emotional manipulation. A heart that leads others astray — or gets lost itself.
symbolism: knight on horseback near water with cup

name: Queen of Cups
uprightMeaning: Throne of deep feeling. The Queen of Cups rules the realm of emotion with compassion — intuitive, perceptive, a safe harbor for any heart.
reversedMeaning: Inner emptiness, moodiness, self-protection. A heart closed to protect itself — or overwhelmed by others' pain.
symbolism: throned queen with cup and water

name: King of Cups
uprightMeaning: Master of the heart's domain. The King of Cups governs emotions with balanced diplomacy — calm under storm, compassion without sentimentality.
reversedMeaning: Emotional manipulation, moodiness, volatility. A ruler who wears the crown but is ruled by the tide.
symbolism: enthroned king with cup and scepter
